KNES - Kinesiology

KNES619 Current Readings in Physical Cultural Studies (1 Credit) Student led presentations of contemporary literature in physical cultural studies and related areas. Emphasis on material describing new empirical and/or theoretical insights, innovative methods, and emerging issues. Repeatable to: 10 credits. Credit Only Granted for: KNES609 or KNES619. Additional Information: Priority enrollment for students in Kinesiology in the Physical Cultural Studies specialization area.

KNES621 Teaching Elementary Physical Education (3 Credits) Students will learn aspects of teaching elementary physical education including: characteristics of elementary school students, testing, grading, teaching lifetime skills, and effective teaching techniques using SHAPE America Standards. Restriction: Must be enrolled in Pre-K to 12 Physical Education Master of Education program. Credit Only Granted for: KNES689V or KNES621. Formerly: KNES689V.

KNES651 Motor Control & Synergy (3 Credits) Students will learn the history, concepts, theories, computations, and pathology regarding motor synergies of human movements. Credit Only Granted for: KNES651 or KNES789P. Formerly: KNES789P .
